verdict_text: Superlattice Tc between parent values; no clear advantage
honest_assessment: The hybrid cuprate-nickelate superlattice route yields phonon-only Tc of ~21 K (mu*=0.10), which is marginal compared to the Hg1223 parent phonon-only Tc of 34 K. The remaining gap to room temperature is 279 K. Any genuine Tc advantage over parent compounds would have to come from non-phonon mechanisms (spin fluctuations, interface charge transfer) not captured by isotropic Eliashberg theory.
